evidences.title: How to Change Timezone on Binance - YouTube
evidences.content: Tap on that to open the settings menu. Change and chart time zone. Tap on it. A list of available time zones will appear. These are sorted by region and UTC offset. Scroll through the list and find the time zone that matches your local time or whichever time zone you prefer for tracking your trades and charts. Once you tap on your desired time zone, it will be instantly applied. No need to manually save or restart the app. From now on, all time stamps across the app, including your trade history and charts, will be displayed according to your selected time zone. And that's all there is to it. If this video helped you, make sure to give it a thumbs up and consider subscribing to our channel for more tutorials just like this one. Thanks for watching. [...] Hello everyone and welcome back to our channel. In today's quick and easy tutorial, I'll walk you through the steps to change the time zone on the Binance mobile app. This is especially useful if your chart data or transaction history doesn't match your local time. How it Works? The script monitors four core VSA components to identify a Climactic Bar: Selling Background: Price must be below the Trend EMA (default 50). Sell Candle: A bearish (Red) candle. Big Spread: The candle's body size must be significantly larger than the average spread. Ultra High Volume: Volume must be substantially higher than the recent average, signaling institutional "climax" activity. The 3 [...] clear "BUY" and "SELL" visual labels.
